title: Fixing TF to < 2.16

functions_modified.function_name: setup invocation / dependency strings in extras_require)
functions_modified.full_name: setup (setuptools.setup invocation / dependency strings in extras_require)

step_plan.summary: Pins TensorFlow to versions below 2.16 in project extras to avoid breakages introduced by TF 2.16+, and updates the TensorFlow regression compatibility test to explicitly enforce/communicate that supported version range.

steps.operation: Update TensorFlow extra requirement to explicitly cap TF to <2.16 (and ensure the cap propagates through the `all` extra if it aggregates `gpu`)
steps.reason: The primary fix for #2073 is to prevent dependency resolution from selecting TensorFlow 2.16+. The authoritative place to enforce this is the TensorFlow requirement string under `extras_require['gpu']` (and any meta-extra like `all` that includes it). Using an explicit `<2.16` constraint makes the support policy clear and keeps installs/CI from pulling incompatible TF versions.
steps.side_effects: Changes the environment produced by `pip install .[gpu]`/`.[all]`, which may alter CI lockstep behavior and downstream user environments. If the project previously pinned `<=2.15.0`, loosening to `<2.16` may allow newer 2.15.x patch releases; verify tests still pass on the highest allowed TF. Packaging checks (sdist/wheel) should be rerun.

steps.operation: Make TensorFlow compatibility test explicitly enforce/communicate the supported TF range (<2.16) via a guard (skip or assert with actionable message)
steps.reason: Even with packaging pins, users/CI can still run the test suite in an environment that already has TF>=2.16 installed. Adding a version guard ensures failures are explained (or the test is skipped) rather than producing confusing downstream API errors, and it codifies the project’s declared support range in tests.
steps.side_effects: Test behavior changes under TF>=2.16 (likely skip or explicit failure). If CI matrices include multiple TF versions, update expectations accordingly. May require importing a version parser (e.g., `packaging.version`) or using `tf.__version__` parsing logic; ensure this does not add unintended heavy dependencies beyond what tests already have.
